message:
feat(ext-conformance): complete fixture corpus for 208 must-pass extensions (bd-1f42.4.2)

220 fixture files covering all 208 must-pass extensions with 742 scenarios.
Every extension has at least one positive (no_error) and one negative (is_error)
scenario. Covers community (58), npm (66), official-pi-mono (40), third-party (23),
agents-mikeastock (1), and base fixture extensions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1elj","title":"Enable markdown feature in rich_rust for assistant response rendering","description":"Enable the markdown feature in rich_rust to properly render assistant responses with formatting.

## Current State
- rich_rust has features = ["full"] but markdown rendering not used
- Assistant responses with markdown (headers, bold, lists, code blocks) render as plain text
- glamour is used for markdown in interactive TUI but not for print mode

## Implementation
1. Ensure 'markdown' feature is enabled in Cargo.toml (check if included in 'full')
2. Use rich_rust::Markdown in print mode output (src/main.rs, src/tui.rs)
3. Ensure PiConsole has method to render markdown content
4. Test with various markdown constructs

## Binary Size Impact
- Adds ~100KB (pulldown-cmark dependency)

## Test Plan
- Unit test: markdown rendering produces expected ANSI output
- Manual test: pi -p 'Write a list of 3 items' shows formatted list

## Files to Modify
- Cargo.toml (verify feature)
- src/tui.rs (add markdown rendering method)
- src/main.rs (use for print mode)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:33.228230583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:29:47.960168833Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:29:47.960105465Z","close_reason":"Completed: print-mode assistant output renders Markdown via rich_rust::Markdown; unit tests added; gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1elj","depends_on_id":"bd-20lm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:33.234790707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

","created_at":"2026-02-05T21:10:56Z"},{"id":482,"issue_id":"bd-1f5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"QuickJS embedder obligations + hardening (key references)

- Microtasks/job queue: QuickJS requires the embedder to drain pending jobs (Promises/queueMicrotask) via JS_ExecutePendingJob. This is the foundation of the PiJS deterministic tick model.
  Ref: https://cs.opensource.google/fuchsia/fuchsia/+/master:third_party/quickjs/quickjs.h

- Module resolution: embedder can install a custom module loader (JS_SetModuleLoaderFunc) to control how import/module names resolve (critical for node:* shims and virtual modules).
  Ref: https://cs.opensource.google/fuchsia/fuchsia/+/master:third_party/quickjs/quickjs.h

- Resource limits: QuickJS supports a memory cap (JS_SetMemoryLimit) and an interrupt hook (JS_SetInterruptHandler) suitable for CPU/time budgets (terminate runaway extensions deterministically).
  Ref: https://cs.opensource.google/fuchsia/fuchsia/+/master:third_party/quickjs/quickjs.h
  Ref (overview + timeout mention): https://quickjs-ng.github.io/quickjs-ng/developer-guide/intro.html

- rquickjs maps these controls into Rust-level APIs (Runtime::set_memory_limit, set_max_stack_size, set_gc_threshold, etc.).
  Ref: https://docs.rs/rquickjs/latest/rquickjs/struct.Runtime.html

Acceptance reminder for bd-1f5: these embedder controls must be exercised in unit tests (budget exceed => deterministic host_result error; cancellation must not silently drop).","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:11:02Z"},{"id":585,"issue_id":"bd-1f5","author":"WhiteWolf","text":"Progress update (WhiteWolf): completed pi.log hostcall plumbing across PiJS + shared dispatcher path.
